my ptpresenters thales triq Composing Different Models of Computation in Kepler and Ptolemy II Antoon Goderis, Christopher Brooks , Ilkay Altintas , Edward A. Lee , Goble Carol Citation Antoon Goderis, Christopher Brooks, Ilkay Altintas, Edward A. Le

, Christopher Brooks, Ilkay Altintas, Edward A. Lee, Goble Carol. "Composing Different Models of Computation in Kepler and Ptolemy II". 2007 Proceedings, International Conference on Computational Science (ICCS), 182-190, May, 2007; A later version ha

nce (ICCS), 182-190, May, 2007; A later version has been published in Future Generation Computer Systems (FGCS), Elsevier. Abstract A model of computation (MoC) is a formal abstraction of execution in a computer. There is a need for composing MoCs in

a computer. There is a need for composing MoCs in e-science. Kepler, which is based on Ptolemy II, is a scientific workflow environment that allows for MoC composition. This paper explains how MoCs are combined in Kepler and Ptolemy II and analyzes

re combined in Kepler and Ptolemy II and analyzes which combinations of MoCs are currently possible and useful. It demonstrates the approach by combining MoCs involving dataflow and finite state machines. The resulting classification should be releva

nes. The resulting classification should be relevant to other workflow environments wishing to combine multiple MoCs. Electronic downloads http://dx.doi.org/10.1007/978-3-540-72588-6_33 . (SpringerLink) http://www.mygrid.org.uk/wiki/bin/viewfile/Mygr
